title: TRIAGE output — blast-radius binding + queue routing for each DP*

triage.description: One entry per DP*; blast-radius binding + queue routing for each point

id.description: DP id carried verbatim from 01-decision-points; never re-minted

decision.description: Decision text carried verbatim from 01; never re-authored

category.description: Category carried verbatim from 01; never re-categorized

candidate_blast_radius.description: DECISION-EXTRACT's proposal (foundational | local), carried for audit

blast_radius.description: BINDING call: exactly foundational | local | trivial

blast_decision.description: confirmed (matches candidate) | overridden (differs); downgrade to trivial always overridden

blast_rationale.description: One line: discriminator reason for binding tag; required when overridden

cut_status.description: in-cut | not-yet for foundational; null for local/trivial

cut_ref.description: Cut element reference carried verbatim from 01

cut_gap.description: True only for in-cut foundational point whose cut_ref is null or deferred:<slice>

defer_to.description: Slice id (e.g. S3) for not-yet foundational; null otherwise

route.description: Exactly one queue assignment: deterministic from blast_radius × cut_status axes

route_rationale.description: One line: why this queue, from blast_radius + cut_status axes

resolution_queue.description: DP ids routed resolution_queue (foundational + in-cut), in triage[] order

slice_deferred.description: Foundational + not-yet DPs; each carries defer_to slice id

defer_to.description: Slice id this DP is deferred to (e.g. S3)

deferred_queue.description: DP ids routed to Phase 3 (local), in triage[] order

conventions.description: Trivial points; no architectural fork, left to standard convention, no ADR owed

note.description: One line: no architectural fork; left to standard convention, no ADR owed

cut_gaps.description: DP ids with cut_gap: true; empty when cut named every in-cut foundational point

triage_counts.description: Walk to count, don't estimate; four sub-counts sum to total
